turns.content: Install the Grafana operator in namespace grafana. Add a wait after creating the namespace to ensure it's ready before creating the OperatorGroup.

I'll create an Ansible playbook to install the Grafana operator in the `grafana` namespace with proper sequencing to ensure the namespace is ready before creating the OperatorGroup.

Here's what I'll automate:
1. Create the `grafana` namespace
2. Wait for namespace to be fully ready
3. Create OperatorGroup in the grafana namespace
4. Create Subscription to install the Grafana operator
5. Wait for operator installation to complete

```yaml
---
- name: Install Grafana Operator
  hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  gather_facts: false
  vars:
    grafana_namespace: grafana
    operator_channel: v4
    
  tasks:
    - name: Create grafana namespace
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        name: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        state: present
        
    - name: Wait for namespace to be ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        name: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Active
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 60
        
    - name: Create OperatorGroup for Grafana
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
          kind: OperatorGroup
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operatorgroup
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            targetNamespaces:
              - "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        state: present
        
    - name: Create Subscription for Grafana Operator
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
          kind: Subscription
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operator
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            channel: "{{ operator_channel }}"
            name: grafana-operator
            source: community-operators
            sourceNamespace: openshift-marketplace
        state: present
        
    - name: Wait for Grafana operator to be ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: apps/v1
        kind: Deployment
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "operators.coreos.com/grafana-operator.grafana"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Available
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 300
        
    - name: Verify operator installation
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
        kind: ClusterServiceVersion
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "operators.coreos.com/grafana-operator.grafana"
      register: csv_info
      
    - name: Display installation status
      debug:
        msg: "Grafana operator {{ csv_info.resources[0].metadata.name }} is {{ csv_info.resources[0].status.phase }}"
      when: csv_info.resources | length > 0
```

This playbook includes the explicit wait after namespace creation and comprehensive verification steps to ensure the operator is fully deployed and operational.
Install the Grafana operator in namespace grafana. Add a wait after creating the namespace to ensure it's ready before creating the OperatorGroup.

I'll create an Ansible playbook to install the Grafana operator in the `grafana` namespace with proper sequencing to ensure the namespace is ready before creating the OperatorGroup.

Here's what I'll automate:
1. Create the `grafana` namespace
2. Wait for namespace to be fully ready
3. Create OperatorGroup in the grafana namespace
4. Create Subscription to install the Grafana operator
5. Wait for operator installation to complete

```yaml
---
- name: Install Grafana Operator
  hosts: localhost
  gather_facts: false
  vars:
    grafana_namespace: grafana
    operator_name: grafana-operator
    
  tasks:
    - name: Create grafana namespace
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        name: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        state: present
        
    - name: Wait for namespace to be ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        name: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Ready
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 120
        
    - name: Create OperatorGroup for Grafana
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
          kind: OperatorGroup
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operatorgroup
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            targetNamespaces:
              - "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        state: present
        
    - name: Create Subscription for Grafana operator
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
          kind: Subscription
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operator
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            channel: v5
            name: grafana-operator
            source: community-operators
            sourceNamespace: openshift-marketplace
        state: present
        
    - name: Wait for Grafana operator to be installed
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
        kind: ClusterServiceVersion
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "operators.coreos.com/grafana-operator.{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Succeeded
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 300
      register: csv_result
      
    - name: Display installation status
      debug:
        msg: "Grafana operator successfully installed in namespace {{ grafana_namespace }}"
      when: csv_result is succeeded
```

This playbook ensures proper sequencing with explicit waits and will verify the operator is fully deployed before completion.
